title: The Ozempic Rebound Looks Catastrophic in Trials. Real-World Data Disagrees.
description: Clinical trials say stopping Ozempic or Mounjaro triggers rapid, near-total weight regain. A Cleveland Clinic study of nearly 8,000 real patients found something strikingly different — and the reason why exposes a fundamental flaw in how we've been reading the trial data all along. The rebound isn't a myth. But the catastrophe narrative may be.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Ozempic Rebound Looks Catastrophic in Trials. Real-World Data Tells a Different Story — and the Gap Is the Story.
